context.project_description: Add token usage tracking to LLM provider classes to log prompt and completion tokens for each query, leveraging existing SDK capabilities and structured logging infrastructure.
key_requirements: Instrument core chat methods (chat, chat_stream) in OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, CohereChat, and OllamaChat providers
Extract and log token usage data from provider-specific response objects using StructuredLogger
Support both prompt_tokens and completion_tokens for each LLM call
Use existing logging infrastructure at fitz_ai/logging/logger.py with structured logging format
constraints: Do not introduce full instrumentation system or global trackers unless absolutely necessary
Leverage existing provider SDK capabilities (response.usage fields, meta.billed_units) rather than reimplementing token counting logic
Only modify actual provider files that exist in the codebase (openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py)
Ensure all providers correctly expose usage data where possible, add fallback logging for missing tokens
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/logging/logger.py
stakeholders: user requesting visibility into LLM token costs

in_scope: instrumenting core chat methods (chat, chat_stream) in provider classes to extract and log prompt/completion token counts
leveraging existing provider-level APIs that return token usage data (e.g., response.usage fields)
using existing StructuredLogger infrastructure for emitting structured logs with token usage metadata
out_of_scope: introducing a full instrumentation system with InstrumentedProxy, BenchmarkHook, or global trackers
adding new abstract protocols or complex hooks beyond direct method instrumentation
modifying non-existent modules or creating new files not present in the codebase
implementing token counting logic from scratch when SDKs already provide usage metadata

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/logging/logger.py — Unified logging setup for the entire Fitz project

assumptions.assumption: The original request only requires simple token cost visibility without need for complex instrumentation systems
assumptions.impact: Over-engineering the solution with global trackers and hooks could add unnecessary complexity, maintenance burden, and potential performance overhead. Simpler direct instrumentation is more aligned with existing architecture.

assumptions.assumption: OpenAI-style token counting logic isn't already implemented in the system
assumptions.impact: If token usage data is already being parsed by some layer (e.g., FitzKragEngine), then reimplementing this logic would be redundant or potentially conflicting with existing code paths.

assumptions.assumption: All LLM provider libraries return structured token usage information in predictable ways
assumptions.impact: If providers like Ollama don't expose built-in usage data, then custom parsing may be required which wasn't anticipated in the design. This could increase implementation complexity or require additional middleware.

assumptions.assumption: Existing logging infrastructure (`StructuredLogger`) can handle structured token logs without modification
assumptions.impact: If the logging system doesn't support structured fields properly, then it will either fail silently or produce unparseable outputs that break downstream dashboards/metrics systems.

assumptions.assumption: Providers such as Ollama do not currently expose token usage metadata and therefore require custom handling
assumptions.impact: Assuming a one-size-fits-all approach across all providers leads to incorrect implementation for those without native support. Without checking actual capabilities per provider, this assumption will lead to incomplete or broken tracking.

approaches.name: Protocol Extension with Structured Responses
approaches.description: Extend base provider protocols to return structured response types that include TokenUsage metadata, updating all concrete implementations to extract and return usage data from API responses.
pros: Maintains backward compatibility through optional usage fields
Leverages existing instrumentation system for aggregation
Follows established abstraction patterns in the codebase
cons: Requires changes across all provider implementations
Initial implementation complexity due to extensive refactoring needed
best_for: Production systems requiring comprehensive token tracking

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Aggregation Layer
approaches.description: Create a wrapper layer that intercepts provider calls through InstrumentedProxy to capture and aggregate usage data by parsing raw responses, without modifying base protocols.
pros: Minimal impact on existing protocol definitions
Can be implemented incrementally per provider type
cons: Cannot access token info if providers already discard it
Requires parsing of raw response formats for each provider
Doesn't integrate cleanly with structured logging patterns
best_for: Systems where protocol modifications are not feasible

architecture.reasoning: This approach is correct because it addresses the root cause: provider implementations currently discard token usage data rather than exposing it. The instrumentation system cannot retroactively extract information that's already been lost, so providers must be modified to return structured responses containing usage metadata from the start. This maintains backward compatibility through optional fields and leverages existing patterns like the protocol abstraction architecture. The alternative approach of using instrumentation alone fails because it can't access data that providers have already discarded in their current string-returning implementations.

key_tradeoffs.Backward Compatibility vs. Comprehensive: Extending provider interfaces with structured responses ensures comprehensive tracking but requires changes across all provider implementations; however, making usage fields optional allows gradual adoption without breaking existing consumers.
key_tradeoffs.Instrumentation-Level Tracking vs. Sourc: Attempting to track token usage at the instrumentation level fails because providers currently discard usage data—thus, the solution must capture usage at the source where it's available.

technology_considerations: Python NamedTuple or dataclass for TokenUsage structure
Protocol-based abstraction in base.py to enforce structured responses across provider implementations
InstrumentedProxy system extension to support numerical aggregation hooks and context propagation
OpenAI client usage parsing to extract total_tokens, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens from API response
Ollama JSON response parsing for eval_count/prompt_eval_count fields during chat operations
architecture.scope_statement: Extend LLM provider protocols to return structured responses including token usage metadata, update all concrete implementations to extract API usage fields, modify Answer objects to include metadata for tracking, and extend API responses to return accumulated query costs.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking for LLM Queries
adrs.context: The user requests token usage tracking to see how many LLM tokens each query costs. Current codebase lacks this capability entirely.
adrs.decision: Extend provider protocols with structured response types that include token usage metadata, update all concrete implementations to extract and return usage data, modify the engine logic to aggregate these values, and extend Answer class to support metadata fields for token tracking.
adrs.rationale: This approach integrates cleanly with existing patterns, leverages the instrumentation system, maintains backward compatibility through optional metadata fields and protocol extension, and provides comprehensive coverage of all LLM operations. It avoids the fundamental issue that simple wrapping cannot help when providers already discard token info in their current string-returning implementations.
consequences: All provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.) must be updated to return structured types instead of basic strings/arrays
Concrete implementation classes (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, OllamaChat, etc.) will need modifications to parse API responses for usage data and construct appropriate response objects
Engine logic in FitzKragEngine will require updates to aggregate token counts from provider responses and store them in Answer metadata
Answer class must be extended to include a metadata field that can hold token usage information
API endpoints (query, chat) need modification to return accumulated token usage in their response objects
Instrumentation system needs enhancements to support tracking of numerical aggregation metrics like token counts
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1: Wrap providers with InstrumentedProxy - rejected because current implementations already discard token data in string returns, so wrapping cannot recover it later
Alternative 2: Modify return types after fact by post-processing responses - rejected because if provider returns str, you cannot later extract metadata from it; change must happen at source level
Alternative 3: Add side-channel tracking without modifying protocols - rejected because this breaks the clean abstraction pattern and creates inconsistent interfaces across different provider types

adrs.title: ADR: Optional Usage Metadata Fields in Protocol Extensions
adrs.context: The protocol extension design requires providers to optionally include usage metadata in structured responses, but this creates ambiguity around what constitutes 'required' vs 'optional' fields in the contract.
adrs.decision: Usage metadata fields are optional with default values that indicate missing data, allowing backward compatibility while enabling instrumentation systems to detect and handle incomplete information gracefully.
adrs.rationale: This approach enables providers with existing implementations to adopt the extension without breaking changes. However, it introduces a failure mode where usage tracking becomes unreliable if providers choose not to implement or incorrectly populate these fields.
consequences: Instrumentation systems must implement fallback logic for missing/invalid metadata
Providers can opt out of providing meaningful usage data without system-wide impact
Data quality and completeness become dependent on provider implementation discipline rather than enforced contract compliance
alternatives_considered: Making all usage fields required with strict validation - would break backward compatibility but ensure complete instrumentation
Introducing a separate metadata-only response format - increases protocol complexity and requires more extensive provider refactoring

components.name: QueryRequest
components.purpose: API request model for query operations with token tracking support
responsibilities: Represent structured API requests including conversation context and query parameters

integration_points: ExternalSystem — OpenAI API v1 for chat, embeddings, vision capabilities
ExternalSystem — Anthropic Claude API for chat and vision processing
ExternalSystem — Cohere API for chat, embeddings, and reranking services
ExternalSystem — Ollama HTTP API for local LLM inference with chat, embedding, rerank, and vision support

artifacts.content:
from typing import NamedTuple

class TokenUsage(NamedTuple):
    total_tokens: int
    prompt_tokens: int | None = None  
    completion_tokens: int | None = None
artifacts.purpose: Defines the TokenUsage data structure to capture LLM token consumption metrics
Extends base provider protocols to return structured responses including token usage metadata
Updates the Answer class to include metadata field for storing token usage and other query metrics
Modifies engine logic to aggregate and store token counts from provider responses

artifacts.content:
async def query(request: QueryRequest) -> QueryResponse:
    answer = engine.answer(query)
    
    token_count = answer.metadata.get('token_usage', 0) if hasattr(answer, 'metadata') else 0
    
    return QueryResponse(
        answer=answer,
        token_usage=token_count
    )
artifacts.purpose: Updates API response to include accumulated token usage in query results

This maintains backward compatibility through optional fields and leverages existing patterns like the protocol abstraction architecture. adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking for LLM Queries
adrs.context: The user requests token usage tracking to see how many LLM tokens each query costs. Current codebase lacks this capability entirely.
adrs.decision: Extend provider protocols with structured response types that include token usage metadata, update all concrete implementations to extract and return usage data, modify the engine logic to aggregate these values, and extend Answer class to support metadata fields for token tracking.
adrs.rationale: This approach integrates cleanly with existing patterns, leverages the instrumentation system, maintains backward compatibility through optional metadata fields and protocol extension, and provides comprehensive coverage of all LLM operations. It avoids the fundamental issue that simple wrapping cannot help when providers already discard token info in their current string-returning implementations.
consequences: All provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.) must be updated to return structured types instead of basic strings/arrays
Concrete implementation classes (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat, OllamaChat, etc.) will need modifications to parse API responses for usage data and construct appropriate response objects
Engine logic in FitzKragEngine will require updates to aggregate token counts from provider responses and store them in Answer metadata
Answer class must be extended to include a metadata field that can hold token usage information
API endpoints (query, chat) need modification to return accumulated token usage in their response objects
Instrumentation system needs enhancements to support tracking of numerical aggregation metrics like token counts
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1: Wrap providers with InstrumentedProxy - rejected because current implementations already discard token data in string returns, so wrapping cannot recover it later
Alternative 2: Modify return types after fact by post-processing responses - rejected because if provider returns str, you cannot later extract metadata from it; change must happen at source level
Alternative 3: Add side-channel tracking without modifying protocols - rejected because this breaks the clean abstraction pattern and creates inconsistent interfaces across different provider types

adrs.title: ADR: Optional Usage Metadata Fields in Protocol Extensions
adrs.context: The protocol extension design requires providers to optionally include usage metadata in structured responses, but this creates ambiguity around what constitutes 'required' vs 'optional' fields in the contract.
adrs.decision: Usage metadata fields are optional with default values that indicate missing data, allowing backward compatibility while enabling instrumentation systems to detect and handle incomplete information gracefully.
adrs.rationale: This approach enables providers with existing implementations to adopt the extension without breaking changes. However, it introduces a failure mode where usage tracking becomes unreliable if providers choose not to implement or incorrectly populate these fields.
consequences: Instrumentation systems must implement fallback logic for missing/invalid metadata
Providers can opt out of providing meaningful usage data without system-wide impact
Data quality and completeness become dependent on provider implementation discipline rather than enforced contract compliance
alternatives_considered: Making all usage fields required with strict validation - would break backward compatibility but ensure complete instrumentation
Introducing a separate metadata-only response format - increases protocol complexity and requires more extensive provider refactoring

class TokenUsage(NamedTuple):

    prompt_tokens: int | None = None  
    completion_tokens: int | None = None

Extends base provider protocols to return structured responses including token usage metadata

async def query(request: QueryRequest) -> QueryResponse:
    answer = engine.answer(query)

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: MVP Implementation
phases.objective: Extend chat provider protocols and implementations to support token usage metadata in responses
deliverables: Updated ChatProvider protocol with new return type supporting metadata including tokens
Modified OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama chat providers to return structured responses with token data
Engine logic updates to collect and aggregate these metrics into answer metadata
key_risks: Not all SDKs return usage fields in response objects (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Structural response format may break existing consumers of chat methods that expect raw strings
Engine cannot reliably extract usage data from various provider response formats
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat; print('OpenAI provider imports OK')"

phases.name: Integration + Testing
phases.objective: Integrate token usage collection into engine logic and validate correctness through comprehensive testing
deliverables: Updated engine logic to collect usage metadata and merge into answer metadata
Unit tests for token extraction across all provider implementations
Test harness demonstrating structured logging with token usage data
key_risks: Anthropic does not expose usage via .usage field but only through metadata dict requiring special handling
Cohere API returns billable units instead of raw token counts requiring conversion logic
Ollama returns tokens in separate fields that aren't standardized across model variants

risks.description: Not all SDKs return `usage` fields in response objects, particularly Anthropic and Cohere which may require special handling of metadata fields.
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific token extraction logic with fallbacks to handle different response formats from each LLM provider.
risks.contingency: If usage data cannot be extracted, log a warning and populate answer metadata with empty token fields to ensure consistency.

risks.description: Cohere API returns billable units instead of raw token counts requiring conversion logic to map billed_units to tokens.
risks.mitigation: Implement a mapping function that converts Cohere's billed_units into standard token counts based on known model mappings or defaults.
risks.contingency: If billing unit to token conversion fails, log an error and proceed with minimal tracking rather than failing the operation.

risks.description: Ollama returns tokens in separate fields that aren't standardized across model variants, such as `prompt_eval_count` and `eval_count`.
risks.mitigation: Create flexible parsing logic to extract token counts from Ollama responses regardless of field name variations by checking for expected keys.
risks.contingency: If required token fields are missing, log a warning and record zero tokens for that provider in the metadata.
risks.verification: assert 'prompt_eval_count' in raw_response and 'eval_count' in raw_response

risks.description: External API behavior changes can silently break usage tracking if fields are moved or renamed (e.g., OpenAI moving usage to metadata).
risks.mitigation: Add comprehensive fallback logic and defensive checks that verify presence of expected token fields before attempting extraction.
risks.contingency: Implement a monitoring hook that flags missing or malformed token data in responses, alerting developers to API drift issues.

risks.description: Token fields may be missing from provider responses leading to silent omission of usage metrics without exception throwing.
risks.mitigation: Enforce that answer metadata always contains token_usage key with default values even when actual counts are unavailable.
risks.contingency: Log a warning indicating missing token data and return placeholder values to maintain API contract integrity.

risks.description: Inconsistent token mapping across providers due to differing definitions of tokens (e.g., OpenAI vs Anthropic).
risks.mitigation: Document provider-specific token behavior and establish clear conversion rules where applicable.
risks.contingency: Implement an integration test that verifies similar queries produce consistent logical token counts when possible or at least predictable variations by provider type.
risks.verification: verify same query results in consistent logical counts (when possible)

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback logic for missing token fields that logs warnings and populates metadata with defaults to ensure API contract integrity.
Add monitoring hooks to flag broken or changed token field structures in LLM responses, allowing early detection of external API drift.

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: MVP Implementation
phases.objective: Extend chat provider protocols and implementations to support token usage metadata in responses
deliverables: Updated ChatProvider protocol with new return type supporting metadata including tokens
Modified OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama chat providers to return structured responses with token data
Engine logic updates to collect and aggregate these metrics into answer metadata
key_risks: Not all SDKs return usage fields in response objects (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Structural response format may break existing consumers of chat methods that expect raw strings
Engine cannot reliably extract usage data from various provider response formats
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat; print('OpenAI provider imports OK')"

phases.name: Integration + Testing
phases.objective: Integrate token usage collection into engine logic and validate correctness through comprehensive testing
deliverables: Updated engine logic to collect usage metadata and merge into answer metadata
Unit tests for token extraction across all provider implementations
Test harness demonstrating structured logging with token usage data
key_risks: Anthropic does not expose usage via .usage field but only through metadata dict requiring special handling
Cohere API returns billable units instead of raw token counts requiring conversion logic
Ollama returns tokens in separate fields that aren't standardized across model variants

risks.description: Not all SDKs return `usage` fields in response objects, particularly Anthropic and Cohere which may require special handling of metadata fields.
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific token extraction logic with fallbacks to handle different response formats from each LLM provider.
risks.contingency: If usage data cannot be extracted, log a warning and populate answer metadata with empty token fields to ensure consistency.

risks.description: Cohere API returns billable units instead of raw token counts requiring conversion logic to map billed_units to tokens.
risks.mitigation: Implement a mapping function that converts Cohere's billed_units into standard token counts based on known model mappings or defaults.
risks.contingency: If billing unit to token conversion fails, log an error and proceed with minimal tracking rather than failing the operation.

risks.contingency: Implement a monitoring hook that flags missing or malformed token data in responses, alerting developers to API drift issues.

risks.contingency: Log a warning indicating missing token data and return placeholder values to maintain API contract integrity.
